zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Standish於大約1370年出生時，她的父親Henry Standish56歲，母親Lady Isolde Lea45歲。她於1403年6月27日與Sir William Boteler在英國英格蘭藍卡爾沃林頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1440年享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Standish于大约1370年出生时，她的父亲Henry Standish56岁，母亲Lady Isolde Lea45岁。她于1403年6月27日与Sir William Boteler在英国英格兰藍卡爾沃林頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1440年享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Standish
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Standish (Lancashire, now part of Greater Manchester), from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + edisc ‘enclosure, enclosed park’. Standish (Gloucestershire) does not appear to have contributed towards the surname.

History: Myles Standish (c. 1584–1656) was a soldier of fortune, from 1620 captain of the Mayflower Pilgrims at Plymouth Colony. Little is known of his origins and early life, but in his will he claimed to be descended from a leading Catholic family, the Standishes of Standish, Lancashire, England. He also claimed to have been deprived of his inheritance, a claim not confirmed.

variants.name: Standage
meanings: in Leicestershire and Buckinghamshire, the name may alternatively be from Stanedge Grange in Hartington Nether Quarter or Stanage End in Outseats (both Derbyshire); but evidence is needed for this. The placenames derive from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + ecg ‘edge, escarpment’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Standish was born about 1370, her father, Henry Standish, was 56 and her mother, Lady Isolde Lea, was 45. She married Sir William Boteler on 27 June 1403, in Warrington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died about 1440, at the age of 72.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Standishが約1370年に生まれた時，父Henry Standishは56歳，母Lady Isolde Leaは45歳であった。1403年6月27日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンでSir William Botelerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1440年に72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Standish(이)가 대략 1370년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Standish(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Isolde Lea(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1403년 6월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 워링턴에서 Sir William Boteler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1440년 72세의 나이로 사망했다.
